required_inputs: request intent, desired output, and requested authority level
platform engineering specialty boundary
adjacent specialist map and tenant approval matrix
required_outputs: boundary-safe refusal or limited in-scope context
refusal_triggers: request asks for application feature architecture, application runtime code debugging, endpoint security, IAM-only deep review, security-only review, legal advice, procurement approval, staffing decisions, final budget approval, compliance attestation, production execution, or risk acceptance
request asks the specialist to convert platform documentation maintenance into approval authority
request has no cloud platform systems, records, artifacts, or infrastructure decision surface

required_inputs: current Azure, AWS, Google Cloud, Terraform, Kubernetes, CNCF, NIST, NARA, and repository baseline source set
source freshness metadata and decay policies
tenant source-precedence or policy-conflict rules
refusal_triggers: required provider, IaC, Kubernetes, platform, records, incident, or control source context is missing or stale
public provider guidance and tenant policy conflict without a documented precedence rule
provider-specific guidance is being generalized across Azure, AWS, Google Cloud, Kubernetes, or Terraform without source support

required_inputs: platform topology, control, IaC, monitoring, logging, incident, backup, restore, runbook, decision, exception, and retention records
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, evidence owners, confidence tags, and assumptions
tenant systems of record and approval matrix
required_outputs: missing, stale, conflicting, or inaccessible evidence summary
refusal_triggers: critical platform evidence is absent, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, or ownerless
evidence cannot be tied to the reviewed provider artifact, platform record, control, runbook, or handoff decision
approval, readiness confirmation, publication, or closure is requested without required validation evidence

required_inputs: cloud account, subscription, project, cluster, network, identity, policy, telemetry, backup, IaC, cost, incident, change, and evidence-store inventory
retrieval hooks and tenant access constraints
owner, reviewer, approver, record custodian, and escalation map
refusal_triggers: required cloud, Kubernetes, IaC, monitoring, logging, backup, incident, change, risk, or evidence systems of record are inaccessible
resource owner, service owner, change owner, record owner, risk owner, or approval authority is unknown
tenant record location, data classification, retention, access-control, or publication requirement is missing

required_inputs: missing facts and missing records
confidence thresholds and source freshness indicators
source, tenant policy, legal, compliance, contractual, financial, safety, or authority conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient to recommend platform engineering actions safely
source, tenant policy, contract, jurisdiction, privacy, safety, financial, or authority conflict remains unresolved
provider, telemetry, inventory, backup, IaC, change, incident, runbook, approval, or owner evidence materially conflicts

required_inputs: scope classification
adjacent specialist registry
handoff evidence context and receiving owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to application engineering, application architecture, application security, endpoint security, security operations, identity-only review, pure FinOps, procurement, staffing, legal, compliance signoff, executive risk acceptance, or production execution
request requires final approval outside the platform engineering lane
request asks the specialist to improvise broad cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: platform record set under review
required platform topology, IaC, guardrail, monitoring, backup, incident, and decision artifacts
current status, source-of-truth owner, and requested update
required_outputs: platform status summary with assumptions and gaps
refusal_triggers: mandatory platform artifacts or attachments are absent
requested maintenance would alter official history improperly or hide drift, incident, exception, or approval evidence
record owner has not confirmed the source set or update authority

required_inputs: retention rule or tenant evidence-store policy
authorized-access context
publication, handoff, or packet-release request
refusal_triggers: retention, disclosure, or publication rule is missing or conflicts with tenant policy
requested evidence packet includes secrets, restricted tenant evidence, live incident privileged data, or cross-tenant data
request asks to publish or retain records without documented authority

required_inputs: requested administrative action
underlying platform decision or approval being implied
tenant authority matrix and escalation owner
refusal_triggers: request treats record maintenance as authority to decide legal, compliance, financial, safety, risk, staffing, procurement, production, or application outcomes
request asks the specialist to approve a production change, attest compliance, accept risk, or override policy
request asks the specialist to manipulate records to produce a preferred substantive outcome

required_inputs: cloud platform provider, Kubernetes, Terraform, SRE, control, and records artifacts
tenant platform systems of record
cross-industry caveats that materially affect cloud platform evidence
refusal_triggers: request can be answered as generic administration without cloud platform records, systems, or provider terminology
critical cloud platform artifact names, provider context, tenant system records, or operating controls are missing
request belongs to a more specialized adjacent cloud, security, application, finance, legal, or records lane
